title: Three Hypotheses for Per-Cell Beta/Gamma Failure

content.finding: Three competing explanations for why per-cell decomposition of beta/gamma destroys performance (53% MI loss)
content.evidence: Entry 010 experiment result: ALIVE-all (per-cell beta/gamma) MI = 0.092 vs ALIVE-alpha-only MI = 0.210

hypotheses.name: (a) Architecturally Wrong
hypotheses.description: Beta/gamma ARE fundamentally global coupling parameters. Per-cell decomposition destroys the coupling mechanism itself. No fix exists within current architecture.
hypotheses.implication: Accept beta/gamma as frozen, advance to Stage 3+

hypotheses.name: (b) Noisy Signal
hypotheses.description: Per-cell beta/gamma are viable but require better adaptation signal. Current resp_z is too noisy at per-cell level for coupling parameters.
hypotheses.implication: Search for better local proxy or use smoothed/filtered signals

hypotheses.name: (c) Wrong Coupling Form
hypotheses.description: Per-cell works but current coupling equation phi = tanh(alpha*x + beta*(neighbors)) requires global beta/gamma by construction. Different coupling form might enable per-cell.
hypotheses.implication: Stage 4 structural change: modify coupling equation itself

implications: If (a): frozen frame floor >0 for current architecture, Stage 4+ required
If (b): more Stage 2 work needed, search space exists
If (c): Stage 4 structural modification could eliminate frozen frame floor
